title: Multi-thread depth on large deals doubled this quarter
anchor: Active opportunities above $250K ACV average 4.2 engaged contacts in Q2, up from 2.1 contacts in Q1.
connect: Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% compared to 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
body: Active opportunities above $250K ACV average 4.2 engaged contacts in Q2, up from 2.1 contacts in Q1. Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% compared to 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
trace: Multi-thread depth signal from Q2 large deals showing 2x improvement in contact engagement, correlating with win rate differential.

title: Champion re-engagement drives stage movement within three weeks
anchor: Nine of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement in April.
connect: Champion contact lapses occurred twice this quarter: April 14 and March 6.
body: Nine of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement in April. Champion contact lapses occurred twice this quarter: April 14 and March 6.
trace: Champion re-engagement events correlating with deal progression, showing 64% of deals advance post-recontact.

title: Sterling deal committee expanded after executive demo
anchor: Buying committee grew from 4 to 7 contacts following the April 18 executive demo, adding CFO and two business-unit leads.
connect: Committees that grow during evaluation historically close at 26% above flat committees.
body: Buying committee grew from 4 to 7 contacts following the April 18 executive demo, adding CFO and two business-unit leads. Committees that grow during evaluation historically close at 26% above flat committees.
trace: Sterling deal specific committee expansion signal with historical context on committee growth correlation to close rates.

title: Q3 pipeline coverage sits at 3.4x quota
anchor: Open Q3 pipeline totals $4.2M versus the $1.24M quota aggregate.
connect: Pipeline with live engagement in the last 14 days represents $3.3M, or 79% of the open total.
body: Open Q3 pipeline totals $4.2M versus the $1.24M quota aggregate. Pipeline with live engagement in the last 14 days represents $3.3M, or 79% of the open total.
trace: Q3 coverage ratio with engagement quality overlay showing active versus stale pipeline composition.

title: Single-threaded deal concentration drops to 22%
anchor: Single-threaded coverage share sits at 22% in Q2 versus 34% in Q1.
connect: Enterprise closes with multi-thread depth of 5+ contacts represent 7 of 9 Q2 wins.
body: Single-threaded coverage share sits at 22% in Q2 versus 34% in Q1. Enterprise closes with multi-thread depth of 5+ contacts represent 7 of 9 Q2 wins.
trace: Single-threading reduction correlating with enterprise close patterns showing deep threading advantage.

title: Mid-market win rate climbs to 27% this quarter
anchor: Q2 mid-market win rate sits at 27.1% on 19 wins from 70 opportunities, up from 19.1% in Q1.
connect: ICP-aligned deals account for 89% of the Q2 win rate improvement.
body: Q2 mid-market win rate sits at 27.1% on 19 wins from 70 opportunities, up from 19.1% in Q1. ICP-aligned deals account for 89% of the Q2 win rate improvement.
trace: Mid-market win rate improvement with ICP alignment as primary driver of the 8-point gain.

title: Champion documentation crosses 50% for first time
anchor: Champion documentation rate reaches 51% of active opportunities in Q2, the first time above 50%.
connect: Deals with named Champions win at 38% compared to 21% for deals without Champion documentation.
body: Champion documentation rate reaches 51% of active opportunities in Q2, the first time above 50%. Deals with named Champions win at 38% compared to 21% for deals without Champion documentation.
trace: Champion field completion milestone with win rate differential showing documentation impact on outcomes.

title: Proposal-stage aging clears in most deals within a month
anchor: Eleven of 14 proposal-stage deals aged beyond 21 days advanced or closed in the last 30 days.
connect: Six of those 11 advances came within 5 days of a re-issued proposal.
body: Eleven of 14 proposal-stage deals aged beyond 21 days advanced or closed in the last 30 days. Six of those 11 advances came within 5 days of a re-issued proposal.
trace: Proposal-stage deal aging resolution with proposal reissue correlation showing document refresh impact.

title: Enterprise proposal-to-close cycle compresses 11 days

connect: Q2 enterprise multi-thread depth averages 4.2 contacts on deals above $250K ACV.
body: Enterprise proposal-to-closed-won median runs at 38 days in Q2, down from 49 days in Q1. Q2 enterprise multi-thread depth averages 4.2 contacts on deals above $250K ACV.

title: CRM activity capture reaches 88% on closed deals

title: Revised outbound sequence converts at 14%

connect: Prior cadence converted at 9%, while inbound-to-meeting sits at 31% over the same window.
body: Outbound sequence-to-meeting conversion runs at 14% since the April 5 cadence revision, reaching 42 meetings from 300 contacts. Prior cadence converted at 9%, while inbound-to-meeting sits at 31% over the same window.

body: Atlas wins 11 of 18 head-to-head deals versus Beacon Systems in Q2, a 61.1% win rate. Trailing four quarters Beacon head-to-head win rate averaged 24%.
